name: Kenny Rankin
abstract: Kenneth Joseph Rankin (February 10, 1940 – June 7, 2009) was an American singer and songwriter in the folk rock and singer-songwriter genres; he was influenced by jazz. Rankin often sang notes which were in a high range to express emotion.

concerts.label: Music of Alan and Marilyn Bergman
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Roy McCurdy
about_quotes.evidence: I started working with Kenny Rankin... He just stood up and sang on this album... we went out and did a few dates like that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Roy McCurdy interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, November 16, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: John Pizzarelli, Jr.
about_quotes.evidence: I had with me the Kenny Rankin album... And so I learned some of these Kenny Rankin songs... And then I learned some Michael Franks songs.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Pizzarelli, Jr. interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000 [interview 1 of 2]
